summary: Conjecture 1.5 from arXiv:1708.07369 asserts that any finite family of graphs containing at least one forest is $k$-nice for infinitely many integers $k$. The source paper itself provides the main supporting evidence via Theorem 1.6 (families containing a star $K_{1,r+1}$ plus only cycle graphs are $k$-nice for infinitely many $k$), and verifies the conjecture for families with a forest of at most 2 edges and for three specific two-graph families of 3-edge graphs. No subsequent paper resolving or disproving the full conjecture was found in the indexed literature after four targeted web searches and a citation-graph check.
search_queries: Ramsey-nice families graphs forest k-nice conjecture arXiv 1708.07369
Aharoni Alon Haxell Hefetz "Ramsey-nice" forest conjecture 2019 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024
"k-nice" forest graphs Ramsey "Conjecture 1.5" OR "nice families" site:arxiv.org
notes: No follow-up resolving this conjecture was found. The Semantic Scholar citation graph for arXiv:1708.07369 lists only 4 citing papers (as of May 2026), none of which address Conjecture 1.5. Danny Hefetz's publication page (one of the paper's co-authors) shows no direct follow-up on Ramsey-nice families after 2018. The conjecture is a weakening of Question 1.1 (which asks for k-nice for all sufficiently large k); even this weaker form appears to remain open.
